instructions.name: accept_challenge
docs: PVP: Challenger accepts the challenge by matching the bet amount (REKT HIM).
TEAM: A participant joins either the creator's side or the opponent's side.
`params.join_creator_side` is only meaningful for TEAM challenges:
- true  → join the creator's side
- false → join the opponent's side
For PVP challenges this field is ignored.

accounts.name: vault
docs: USDC vault token account — owned by the challenge PDA

accounts.name: challenger_usdc_account
docs: Challenger's USDC token account (source of the matching bet)

accounts.name: usdc_mint
docs: USDC mint — validated by token account constraints above

instructions.name: cancel_challenge
docs: Creator cancels an unaccepted challenge and reclaims their bet.

accounts.name: vault
docs: USDC vault token account — owned by the challenge PDA

accounts.name: creator_usdc_account
docs: Creator's USDC token account (refund destination)

accounts.name: usdc_mint
docs: USDC mint — validated by token account constraints above

instructions.name: claim_winnings
docs: TEAM mode only: a winner on the winning side claims their proportional
share of the pot after the challenge has been settled.
Each participant can only call this once (enforced by the ClaimRecord PDA).

accounts.name: participant
docs: The participant claiming their winnings

accounts.name: vault
docs: USDC vault token account — owned by the challenge PDA

accounts.name: participant_usdc_account
docs: Participant's USDC token account (payout destination)

accounts.name: claim_record
docs: Claim record — created here; its existence prevents double-claiming.
Seeds: [b"claim", challenge.key(), participant.key()]

accounts.name: usdc_mint
docs: USDC mint

instructions.name: create_challenge
docs: Creator posts a new challenge, locking their SOL bet into escrow.
Specify `challenge_type = ChallengeType::Pvp` for a 1-vs-1 challenge or
`ChallengeType::Team` for a multi-participant team challenge.

accounts.name: creator_counter
docs: Per-creator counter — init on first challenge, increment thereafter

accounts.name: challenge
docs: The challenge account itself

accounts.name: vault
docs: USDC vault token account — an ATA owned by the challenge PDA.
Holds all bets (creator's + all participants').

accounts.name: creator_usdc_account
docs: Creator's USDC token account (source of the bet)

accounts.name: usdc_mint
docs: USDC mint — must match the devnet USDC address

instructions.name: settle_challenge
docs: Admin/oracle settles the challenge after expiry.
PVP: funds are sent directly to the winner's USDC account.
TEAM: the winning side is recorded on-chain; each winner must call
`claim_winnings` separately to pull their proportional share.

accounts.name: admin
docs: The admin/oracle wallet that is authorised to settle challenges.
In production this should be a multisig or a Switchboard/Pyth oracle CPI.

accounts.name: challenger
docs: For TEAM challenges pass any pubkey (e.g. creator again) — it won't be paid out here.

accounts.name: vault
docs: USDC vault token account — owned by the challenge PDA

accounts.name: winner_usdc_account
docs: PVP only: winner's USDC token account (creator or challenger depending on outcome).
For TEAM challenges pass any valid USDC token account — it won't be written to.

accounts.name: treasury_usdc_account
docs: Platform treasury USDC token account (receives the fee for both PVP and TEAM)

accounts.name: usdc_mint
docs: USDC mint — validated by token account constraints above

errors.name: BetTooSmall
errors.msg: Bet amount is below the minimum allowed

errors.name: DurationTooShort
errors.msg: Challenge duration is too short (minimum 5 minutes)

errors.name: DurationTooLong
errors.msg: Challenge duration is too long (maximum 7 days)

errors.name: AlreadyAccepted
errors.msg: Challenge has already been accepted

errors.name: NotOpen
errors.msg: Challenge is not in Open status

errors.name: NotActive
errors.msg: Challenge is not in Active status

errors.name: NotExpired
errors.msg: Challenge has not yet expired

errors.name: ChallengeExpired
errors.msg: Challenge has already expired; cannot accept

errors.name: CannotAcceptOwnChallenge
errors.msg: Creator cannot accept their own challenge

errors.name: UnauthorizedCancel
errors.msg: Only the challenge creator can cancel

errors.name: UnauthorizedSettle
errors.msg: Only the admin/oracle can settle challenges

errors.name: Overflow
errors.msg: Arithmetic overflow

errors.name: AssetTooLong
errors.msg: Asset symbol too long (max 10 chars)

errors.name: InvalidResolvesAt
errors.msg: Resolve time must be after expiry time

errors.name: TeamFull
errors.msg: Team is full; max_team_size has been reached

errors.name: AlreadyJoined
errors.msg: You have already joined this challenge

errors.name: WrongChallengeType
errors.msg: This operation is not valid for the challenge type (PVP vs TEAM mismatch)

errors.name: AlreadyClaimed
errors.msg: Winnings have already been claimed for this challenge

errors.name: NotAWinner
errors.msg: You are not on the winning team

errors.name: NotSettled
errors.msg: Challenge has not been settled yet

errors.name: NoWinningSide
errors.msg: The winning side has not been determined (no participants on one side)

types.name: AcceptChallengeParams
docs: `side` is only meaningful for TEAM challenges:
- `true`  → join the **creator's** side
- `false` → join the **opponent's** side
For PVP challenges `side` is ignored; the caller becomes the single challenger.

fields.name: join_creator_side
docs: TEAM only: which side to join (true = creator's side, false = opponent's side).
Ignored for PVP.

types.name: ChallengeAccount
docs: The main challenge account stored on-chain.
PVP seeds:  [b"challenge", creator.key(), challenge_id.to_le_bytes()]
TEAM seeds: same — challenge_type differentiates behaviour at runtime.

fields.name: creator
docs: The wallet that created the challenge

fields.name: challenger
docs: PVP only: the single wallet that accepted the challenge (zero if not yet accepted)

fields.name: creator_team
docs: TEAM only: participants who joined the creator's side (creator is implicitly included)

fields.name: opponent_team
docs: TEAM only: participants who joined the opponent's side

fields.name: max_team_size
docs: TEAM only: maximum participants per side (0 = no limit up to 50)

fields.name: winning_side
docs: TEAM only: which side won (set during settle_challenge)

fields.name: challenge_type
docs: Whether this is a PVP or TEAM challenge

fields.name: challenge_id
docs: Unique numeric ID for this challenge (per creator)

fields.name: asset
docs: Asset symbol, e.g. "BTC", "SOL", "ETH"

fields.name: bet_amount
docs: Bet amount in USDC micro-units per participant (both sides must match per person)

fields.name: target_price_usd_cents
docs: Target price in USD cents (e.g. $66,500 → 6_650_000)

fields.name: direction
docs: Direction of the creator's prediction

fields.name: expires_at
docs: Unix timestamp when the challenge expires (no more accepts/joins after this)

fields.name: resolves_at
docs: Unix timestamp when the price is evaluated (end of prediction window)

fields.name: status
docs: Current status

fields.name: vault_bump
docs: PDA bump for the vault

fields.name: bump
docs: PDA bump for this account

types.name: ChallengeStatus
docs: On-chain status of a challenge

types.name: ChallengeType
docs: Whether the challenge is 1-vs-1 or team-based

types.name: ClaimRecord
docs: Tracks whether a TEAM participant has already claimed their winnings.
Seeds: [b"claim", challenge.key(), participant.key()]
Created (init) when the participant calls claim_winnings for the first time.
Its existence is the double-claim guard.

fields.name: asset
docs: Asset symbol, e.g. "BTC"

fields.name: bet_amount
docs: Bet amount in USDC micro-units per participant (6 decimals, e.g. $5 = 5_000_000)

fields.name: target_price_usd_cents
docs: Target price in USD cents (e.g. $66,500 → 6_650_000)

fields.name: direction_above
docs: true = creator predicts price will be ABOVE target; false = BELOW

fields.name: expires_at
docs: Unix timestamp: last moment another user can accept / join this challenge

fields.name: resolves_at
docs: Unix timestamp: when the price outcome is evaluated

fields.name: challenge_type
docs: PVP = single opponent; Team = any number of participants per side

fields.name: max_team_size
docs: TEAM only: max participants per side (0 = no limit, capped at MAX_TEAM_SIZE = 50).
Ignored for PVP challenges.

types.name: CreatorCounter
docs: A per-creator counter so each creator can have multiple challenges.
Seeds: [b"creator_counter", creator.key()]

types.name: PredictionDirection
docs: Direction of the price prediction

types.name: WinningSide
docs: Which side won a TEAM challenge (stored after settlement so claim_winnings can verify)
